assertion_results.evidence: The output slugify.ts contains none of the four removed elements: the TICKET-4821/incident block (lines 1-4 of the original) is absent, and all three restatement one-liners ('lowercase the title', 'replace runs of non-alphanumeric characters with a single hyphen', 'strip leading and trailing hyphens') are gone. The NFKD kernel is preserved and tightened: '// Use NFKD (not NFC) to decompose combining marks into separate code points, allowing the regex to strip diacritics; NFC would keep "é" as a single point.' The agent wrote the file directly (Write tool, ordinal 6) rather than only advising removal.

Both legitimate comments survive in the written output (ordinal 6). The JSDoc remains intact: '/** Convert a human-readable title into a URL-safe slug. Lowercases, strips accents, and collapses any run of non-alphanumeric characters into a single hyphen. */'. The NFKD kernel is retained and tightened: '// Use NFKD (not NFC) to decompose combining marks into separate code points, allowing the regex to strip diacritics; NFC would keep "é" as a single point.' The final_message also explicitly states: 'The JSDoc comment remains — it's concise, explains intent, and is exactly what an API consumer needs' and describes consolidating (not removing) the NFKD explanation.

final_message ends with 'You can now **merge** this into your branch, **push and open a PR**, **leave as-is**, or make other changes. It’s your call — I’ve verified and reviewed; integration is yours to decide.' No merge, push, or PR tool invocation appears in the run record.

meta_results.evidence: The agent's final_message follows the skill's three-phase finishing sequence exactly: it invoked code-review (Phase 1), ran and presented test output as evidence (Phase 2), then surfaced integration options — 'merge this into your branch, push and open a PR, leave as-is, or make other changes. It's your call — I've verified and reviewed; integration is yours to decide' — which mirrors the skill's Phase 3 language ('state that the work is reviewed and verified, then offer the user choices such as merge, push/open PR, leave as-is') and its 'Don't Finish the Branch Unilaterally' rule.
